key_insight: The paper reframes constructing counterexamples to Kalai’s algebraic Hirsch conjecture as a sparse-reward graph search problem and solves it with hierarchical reinforcement learning.
engineering_relevance: It shows how hierarchical RL plus equivariant graph neural networks can make extremely sparse, combinatorial search tasks more tractable in computational mathematics.
method: Use a constrained options-based HRL framework to learn temporal abstractions.
Encode graph states with an equivariant GNN policy to respect problem symmetries.
Optimize search over graphs under sparse reward signals to guide counterexample construction.
evidence_signal: The authors evaluate the method across a wide range of degrees and report consistent performance gains over classical RL algorithms and greedy search.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated on a specialized mathematical search benchmark, so generalization to other algebraic conjectures or larger instances may require careful constraint design and validation.
paper_title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Sparse-Reward Search in Commutative Algebra
